symptoms.symptom_name: Chronic superficial scaly, crusted patches in a seborrhoeic distribution (face, scalp, upper chest)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: The seborrhoeic distribution and chronic superficial scaling are typical of pemphigus foliaceus, though shared with seborrhoeic dermatitis.
Same distribution — the reason this disorder is chronically mislabelled as seborrhoeic dermatitis.

symptoms.symptom_name: Acute flare with burning and rapid spread

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Acute exacerbations of pemphigus foliaceus can spread rapidly across the seborrhoeic areas.
Rapid painful spread is a feature of disseminated HSV that mandates exclusion.

symptoms.symptom_name: Weeping of clear fluid with yellowish (non honey-coloured) crust

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Superficial erosions weep and crust; the crust is typically not the thick honey-colour of impetigo.
Absence of thick honey-coloured purulent crust makes classic impetigo less likely, though secondary infection can coexist.
A positive Nikolsky sign indicates a fragile intraepidermal split characteristic of pemphigus; sealed clinician examination finding not obtainable via telehealth.
A positive Nikolsky sign points to a primary blistering process rather than superficial bacterial crusting.

symptoms.symptom_name: Complete sparing of oropharyngeal and conjunctival mucosa

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Mucosal sparing is characteristic (desmoglein-1 is not the dominant mucosal adhesion molecule) and distinguishes pemphigus foliaceus from pemphigus vulgaris.
SJS characteristically involves mucosae; complete mucosal sparing argues strongly against it. Patient-reported absence is reassuring but professional inspection is the gold standard.

symptoms.symptom_name: Absence of punched-out monomorphic erosions / grouped vesicles

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: The characteristic punched-out erosions of eczema herpeticum are absent on examination; viral PCR would be confirmatory if any doubt.

symptoms.symptom_name: Absence of intact tense or flaccid bullae

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: In pemphigus foliaceus the superficial blisters are so fragile they rupture almost immediately, leaving crusted erosions rather than intact bullae.
Systemic toxicity usually accompanies SCAR; its absence is reassuring but not exclusionary without examination.
Disseminated HSV is often febrile; afebrility lowers but does not remove the risk.
Absence of tender regional nodes lowers the likelihood of significant secondary bacterial infection; requires palpation.

symptoms.symptom_name: Aggravation by over-the-counter anti-dandruff / keratolytic shampoos

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Mechanical/chemical irritation of an already fragile superficial epidermis worsens acantholytic disease; also explains treatment failure when mislabelled as dandruff.

symptoms.symptom_name: Skin biopsy with direct immunofluorescence and anti-desmoglein-1 serology

investigation_details.investigation_name: Skin biopsy (lesional H&E + perilesional direct immunofluorescence) and anti-desmoglein-1 antibody serology
investigation_details.expected_result: Subcorneal acantholysis on histology; intercellular IgG/C3 in the superficial epidermis on DIF; positive anti-desmoglein-1 antibodies
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Diagnosis remains presumptive; long-term autoimmune management cannot be safely directed without confirmation.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Direct immunofluorescence showing intercellular IgG in the superficial epidermis plus anti-desmoglein-1 antibodies is diagnostic; never available in real time via telehealth.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Positive Nikolsky sign + mucosal sparing + fragile non-intact blisters together characterise a superficial pemphigus and exclude the infective/drug mimics — but every constituent is a clinician-only examination finding.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Urgent in-person dermatological examination (including Nikolsky sign and full mucosal inspection)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: The discriminating findings that separate a benign autoimmune flare from eczema herpeticum, impetigo and SJS are all physical-examination based and cannot be obtained via telehealth.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Skin biopsy with direct immunofluorescence + anti-desmoglein-1 serology
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Definitive confirmation of pemphigus foliaceus and to direct long-term management; arranged via dermatology referral, not an emergency test.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Viral swab / HSV PCR of a lesion
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: If eczema herpeticum cannot be excluded on examination, HSV PCR guides urgent antiviral therapy.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Bacterial swab (MC&S) of a weeping lesion
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: To confirm and target secondary bacterial infection if suspected clinically; not to be treated empirically without assessment.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Empirical broad-spectrum systemic antibiotics without examination or swab
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Treating presumptively as impetigo before the diagnosis is examined risks masking a dangerous mimic and is an error of commission in this remote setting.
